questions.question: The runs scored by a cricketer in 7 matches are: 45, 50, 0, 102, 34, 50, 25. Find the mean score.
If the mean of five observations x, x+2, x+4, x+6, and x+8 is 11, find the value of x.
Find the median of the data: 31, 37, 35, 38, 42, 23, 17, 18, 35.
A data set has a mean of 15. If every number in the set is multiplied by 3, what will be the new mean?
Find the mode of the following data: 12, 14, 12, 16, 15, 13, 14, 18, 19, 12, 14, 15, 16, 14, 12.
questions.answer: 12 appears 4 times, 14 appears 4 times. The data is bimodal with modes 12 and 14.

questions.question: The heights (in cm) of 10 students are: 143, 148, 135, 150, 128, 139, 149, 146, 151, 132. Find the range.
What is the difference between a Bar Graph and a Histogram?
questions.answer: Bar graphs use discrete categories with spaces between bars; histograms use continuous data intervals with no spaces.

questions.question: Find the median of the first six even numbers.
key_concepts: median for even number of observations

questions.question: The mean of 10 observations was found to be 40. Later it was discovered that 50 was misread as 40. Find the correct mean.
questions.answer: Original sum = 400. New sum = 400 - 40 + 50 = 410. Correct mean = 410 / 10 = 41.

questions.question: In a data set of 15 observations, if the median is 20, how many observations are smaller than 20?
Calculate the mean of the factors of 12.
What is the probability of picking a Sunday in a week?
If the range of a data set is 15 and the smallest value is 24, what is the largest value?
Why is the median sometimes a better measure of central tendency than the mean?
questions.answer: Because the median is not affected by extreme values (outliers), whereas the mean is.

questions.question: A die is thrown once. What is the probability of getting a prime number?
questions.answer: Prime numbers on a die: 2, 3, 5. Total outcomes = 6. Probability = 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.question: A coin is tossed 100 times and heads appeared 45 times. What is the probability of getting a tail?
Find the median of: 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50.
questions.answer: Number of terms = 9. Middle term is the 5th term = 30.

questions.question: What is the sum of probabilities of all possible outcomes in an experiment?
Calculate the mean weight: 35 kg, 40 kg, 45 kg, 50 kg, 55 kg.
The mode of a set of data is 25. If 5 is added to each observation, what will be the new mode?
In a double bar graph, how are the two bars for a single category positioned?
questions.answer: They are placed side-by-side without any gap between them.

questions.question: What is an 'outlier'?
questions.answer: A value that is significantly higher or lower than the rest of the observations in a data set.

questions.question: Find the range of first 10 prime numbers.
If you spin a wheel with 3 red sectors and 5 blue sectors, what is the probability of landing on red?
